hard_gates: Output includes request_fingerprint_binding, approved_context_inventory, quality_review_intake, work_card_packet, text_boundary, and boundary_review_note.
The raw work request is not persisted or reconstructed.
No source text, source chunk, copied excerpt, NotebookLM answer, private payload, secret, credential, runtime absolute path, or _workspaces content appears.
Approved source card refs, index refs, source refs, page refs, claim ceilings, lifecycle states, route hints, and owner decision refs are inventoried by stable ref only.
All supplied optional context refs are preserved by stable ref when present.
Blocked page p:005 is excluded from source-supported work-card refs.
Manual-review page p:003 remains visibly marked.
Deterministic IDs are derived from the fingerprint and sorted ref/page set.
The route surface includes owner_decision_packet_v0 and post_development_review_gate_v0 when owner/post-review follow-up may be needed.
The output explicitly denies owner approval, source truth, answer truth, canon promotion, default-route switch, and project execution authority.
selection_rule: Choose the lowest-token candidate that passes all hard gates unless quality differs by at least 10 points or a boundary blocker exists.
critical_failure_conditions: Any owner approval, canon, source truth, answer truth, default-route, or project execution authority is claimed.
Blocked pages are treated as source-supported work-card refs.
The required output shape is missing.
Supplied optional context refs are silently dropped.
